commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1fedbcb1f92d96c66711e620d76937e1385a1aec)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the native-host guard from Kimi web-search base URL fallback and updates the regression test so custom Moonshot provider base URLs are inherited.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests 0. Total 0 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main rejects non-native Moonshot provider baseUrls and falls back to the default Kimi host. The PR body now adds after-fix live output for one custom proxy, but the linked issue's exact config remains incomplete.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer compatibility/auth-provider decision is needed before changing the fallback semantics; this is not a safe automated repair target.

target.title: fix(moonshot): inherit custom baseUrl for kimi web search
target.body:
Closes #94979

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with a custom Moonshot API proxy or non-default host configured via `models.providers.moonshot.baseUrl` would get `401 Invalid Authentication` from Kimi web search, even though the same API key worked correctly for Moonshot chat completions.

## Why This Change Was Made

`resolveKimiBaseUrl` previously discarded non-native Moonshot base URLs by applying an `isNativeMoonshotBaseUrl()` guard — a function designed for streaming-usage-compat detection, not for validating whether a host should be inherited. When users configured a custom base URL (proxy, private deployment, etc.), the web search provider silently fell back to `api.moonshot.ai/v1`, sending API keys to the wrong endpoint.

Removing this guard aligns Kimi with the existing convention used by two other bundled providers:

- **Ollama** (`extensions/ollama/src/web-search-provider.ts:99-111`): unconditional fallback from `webSearch.baseUrl` → `models.providers.ollama.baseUrl` → default.
- **Gemini** (`extensions/google/src/gemini-web-search-provider.ts:88-116`): unconditional fallback from `webSearch.baseUrl` → `models.providers.google.baseUrl` → default, documented at `docs/tools/gemini-search.md:106`.

Neither applies a native-host guard. No compatibility issues have been reported for those providers. This change is not a design departure — it removes an inconsistency that made Moonshot the only bundled provider refusing to inherit a user-configured chat base URL.

Explicit `tools.web.search.kimi.baseUrl` overrides are unaffected; they take priority before this fallback.

## User Impact

Users with a custom Moonshot base URL no longer need to duplicate it into `tools.web.search.kimi.baseUrl`. Kimi web search will now authenticate successfully using the same `models.providers.moonshot.baseUrl` that already works for model chat completions.

## Evidence

- **Root cause confirmed** by inspecting `isNativeMoonshotBaseUrl` (`extensions/moonshot/provider-catalog.ts:14`), which delegates to `supportsNativeStreamingUsageCompat` — a streaming capability check unrelated to host validation.
- **15/15 tests pass**, including the updated regression test that now asserts custom proxy URLs are correctly inherited.
- **Test output:** `pnpm test extensions/moonshot/src/kimi-web-search-provider.test.ts` → `1 passed, 15 tests, 310ms`.
- **Diff:** 2 files, +3/-3 lines. No changes to core, Telegram, config schema, or `isNativeMoonshotBaseUrl` itself (still used correctly in onboarding setup).
- **Consistency with Ollama and Gemini** confirmed by source inspection; both unconditionally inherit `models.providers.*.baseUrl` without a native-host guard.
- **End-to-end real behavior proof using a third-party Moonshot-compatible proxy** (`https://api.genai.gd.edu.kg/moonshot/v1`) as a custom `models.providers.moonshot.baseUrl`, with no explicit `tools.web.search.kimi.baseUrl` configured. Verified via OpenClaw gateway (built from this branch):

```
=== Configuration === models.providers.moonshot.baseUrl: https://api.genai.gd.edu.kg/moonshot/v1 (custom 3rd-party proxy) tools.web.search.kimi.baseUrl: NOT configured (inherits from moonshot.baseUrl) tools.web.search.provider: kimi

=== Step 1: resolveKimiBaseUrl === Configured moonshot.baseUrl: https://api.genai.gd.edu.kg/moonshot/v1 Inherited baseUrl: https://api.genai.gd.edu.kg/moonshot/v1 Status: PASS - custom baseUrl inherited correctly

=== Step 2: web_search via OpenClaw runtime === web_search RESULT: SUCCESS Provider: kimi Model: kimi-k2.6 Time: 24361ms Has content: YES Content preview: Based on my search, I found the OpenClaw GitHub repository...

=== Gateway Log ===

deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash model configured, enabled automatically. agent model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash (thinking=high, fast=off) http server listening (8 plugins...) ready
```

This reproduces the exact scenario from issue #94979: the API key would previously return HTTP 401 (silently routed to `api.moonshot.ai` by the native-host guard), and after this fix it successfully returns grounded search results through the inherited custom base URL.

---

**AI-assisted:** Built with opencode. Analysis of issue #94979, code-level root cause identification, fix implementation, and test update were AI-assisted with human review and validation.
target.author: yangxiansheng

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
